#!/bin/bash
# Enhanced colorful statusline with human-readable formatting

# Configurable threshold (default: 160K tokens = 80% of 200K context)
THRESHOLD=${CLAUDE_AUTO_COMPACT_THRESHOLD:-160000}

# Parse command line arguments for transcript file override
TRANSCRIPT_OVERRIDE=""
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case $1 in
        --transcript-file)
            TRANSCRIPT_OVERRIDE="$2"
            shift 2
            ;;
        *)
            shift
            ;;
    esac
done

# Read JSON input from stdin
input=$(cat)

# Color definitions (using printf for consistent terminal compatibility)
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
WHITE='\033[0;37m'
BOLD='\033[1m'
DIM='\033[2m'
RESET='\033[0m'

# Helper functions for common extractions
get_model_name() { echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name'; }
get_current_dir() { echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir'; }
get_project_dir() { echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.project_dir'; }
get_session_id() { echo "$input" | jq -r '.session_id'; }
get_version() { echo "$input" | jq -r '.version'; }
get_cost() { echo "$input" | jq -r '.cost.total_cost_usd // "0"'; }
get_duration() { echo "$input" | jq -r '.cost.total_duration_ms // "0"'; }
get_lines_added() { echo "$input" | jq -r '.cost.total_lines_added // "0"'; }
get_lines_removed() { echo "$input" | jq -r '.cost.total_lines_removed // "0"'; }

# Format cost in human-readable way
format_cost() {
    local cost="$1"
    if [[ "$cost" == "null" ]] || [[ -z "$cost" ]] || [[ "$cost" == "0" ]]; then
        printf "${DIM}--${RESET}"
        return
    fi
    
    # Convert to cents for easier handling
    local cents=$(printf "%.0f" "$(echo "$cost * 100" | bc -l 2>/dev/null || echo "0")")
    
    if [[ $cents -eq 0 ]]; then
        printf "${DIM}<1¢${RESET}"
    elif [[ $cents -lt 100 ]]; then
        printf "${YELLOW}%d¢${RESET}" "$cents"
    elif [[ $cents -lt 10000 ]]; then  # Less than $100
        printf "${YELLOW}\$%.2f${RESET}" "$(echo "scale=2; $cents/100" | bc -l)"
    else
        printf "${RED}\$%.2f${RESET}" "$(echo "scale=2; $cents/100" | bc -l)"
    fi
}

# Format duration in human-readable way
format_duration() {
    local ms="$1"
    if [[ "$ms" == "null" ]] || [[ -z "$ms" ]] || [[ "$ms" == "0" ]]; then
        printf "${DIM}--${RESET}"
        return
    fi
    
    local seconds=$(echo "scale=1; $ms/1000" | bc -l 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    
    # Handle different time ranges with appropriate colors and units
    if (( $(echo "$seconds < 1" | bc -l) )); then
        printf "${GREEN}%dms${RESET}" "$ms"
    elif (( $(echo "$seconds < 60" | bc -l) )); then
        if (( $(echo "$seconds < 10" | bc -l) )); then
            printf "${GREEN}%.1fs${RESET}" "$seconds"
        else
            printf "${YELLOW}%.0fs${RESET}" "$seconds"
        fi
    elif (( $(echo "$seconds < 3600" | bc -l) )); then  # Less than 1 hour
        local minutes=$(echo "scale=1; $seconds/60" | bc -l)
        printf "${YELLOW}%.1fm${RESET}" "$minutes"
    else  # 1 hour or more
        local hours=$(echo "scale=1; $seconds/3600" | bc -l)
        printf "${RED}%.1fh${RESET}" "$hours"
    fi
}

# Calculate tokens from transcript
calculate_tokens() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local total_tokens=0

    if [ -n "$session_id" ] && [ "$session_id" != "null" ]; then
        local transcript_path
        if [ -n "$TRANSCRIPT_OVERRIDE" ]; then
            transcript_path="$TRANSCRIPT_OVERRIDE"
        else
            transcript_path=$(find ~/.claude/projects -name "${session_id}.jsonl" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
        fi

        if [ -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
            # Estimate tokens (rough approximation: 1 token per 4 characters)
            local total_chars=$(wc -c < "$transcript_path")
            total_tokens=$((total_chars / 4))
        fi
    fi

    echo "$total_tokens"
}

# Format token count with K notation and color
format_tokens() {
    local total_tokens="$1"
    local percentage="$2"

    if [ "$total_tokens" -eq 0 ]; then
        printf "${DIM}0${RESET}"
        return
    fi

    # Format with K notation
    local token_display
    if [ $total_tokens -ge 1000 ]; then
        token_display=$(echo "scale=1; $total_tokens / 1000" | bc)"K"
    else
        token_display="$total_tokens"
    fi

    # Color based on percentage
    if [ $percentage -ge 90 ]; then
        printf "${RED}🪙 %s${RESET}" "$token_display"
    elif [ $percentage -ge 70 ]; then
        printf "${YELLOW}🪙 %s${RESET}" "$token_display"
    else
        printf "${GREEN}🪙 %s${RESET}" "$token_display"
    fi
}

# Extract session topic from first user message in transcript
get_session_topic() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local max_length=50

    if [ -z "$session_id" ] || [ "$session_id" == "null" ]; then
        printf "${DIM}--${RESET}"
        return
    fi

    local transcript_path
    if [ -n "$TRANSCRIPT_OVERRIDE" ]; then
        transcript_path="$TRANSCRIPT_OVERRIDE"
    else
        transcript_path=$(find ~/.claude/projects -name "${session_id}.jsonl" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
    fi

    if [ ! -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
        printf "${DIM}--${RESET}"
        return
    fi

    # Extract first user message from JSONL transcript (skip "Warmup")
    # Format: {"type": "user", "message": {"role": "user", "content": "..."}}
    # Filter out command-related noise from slash commands and local commands
    # Use perl for multiline XML tag removal since sed doesn't handle multiline well
    # Also skip JSON structures (tool results, arrays, objects) and JSON properties
    local first_message=$(jq -r 'select(.type == "user") | .message.content // empty' "$transcript_path" 2>/dev/null | \
        grep -iv "^warmup$" | \
        perl -0777 -pe 's/<command-message>.*?<\/command-message>//gs; s/<command-name>.*?<\/command-name>//gs; s/<command-args>.*?<\/command-args>//gs; s/<local-command-stdout>.*?<\/local-command-stdout>//gs' | \
        grep -iv "^Caveat: The messages below were generated" | \
        sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//' | \
        grep -v '^\[' | \
        grep -v '^{' | \
        grep -v '^}' | \
        grep -v '^]' | \
        grep -Ev '^"(type|text|tool_use_id|content|is_error)"' | \
        grep -v '^$' | \
        head -1 | tr -d '\n')

    if [ -z "$first_message" ]; then
        printf "${DIM}--${RESET}"
        return
    fi

    # Truncate and add ellipsis if needed
    if [ ${#first_message} -gt $max_length ]; then
        first_message="${first_message:0:$max_length}..."
    fi

    printf "${MAGENTA}%s${RESET}" "$first_message"
}

# Use the helpers
MODEL=$(get_model_name)
DIR=$(get_current_dir)
SESSION_ID=$(get_session_id)
RAW_DURATION=$(get_duration)

# Calculate token information
TOTAL_TOKENS=$(calculate_tokens "$SESSION_ID")
PERCENTAGE=$((TOTAL_TOKENS * 100 / THRESHOLD))
if [ $PERCENTAGE -gt 100 ]; then
    PERCENTAGE=100
fi

# Format the values
DURATION_FORMATTED=$(format_duration "$RAW_DURATION")
TOKENS_FORMATTED=$(format_tokens "$TOTAL_TOKENS" "$PERCENTAGE")
TOPIC_FORMATTED=$(get_session_topic "$SESSION_ID")

# Show git branch if in a git repo with enhanced formatting
GIT_BRANCH=""
if git rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null)
    if [ -n "$BRANCH" ]; then
        # Color branch based on name patterns
        if [[ "$BRANCH" == "main" ]] || [[ "$BRANCH" == "master" ]]; then
            GIT_BRANCH=" ${DIM}|${RESET} ${GREEN}⎇ ${BRANCH}${RESET}"
        elif [[ "$BRANCH" == feature/* ]] || [[ "$BRANCH" == feat/* ]]; then
            GIT_BRANCH=" ${DIM}|${RESET} ${BLUE}⎇ ${BRANCH}${RESET}"
        elif [[ "$BRANCH" == hotfix/* ]] || [[ "$BRANCH" == fix/* ]]; then
            GIT_BRANCH=" ${DIM}|${RESET} ${RED}⎇ ${BRANCH}${RESET}"
        else
            GIT_BRANCH=" ${DIM}|${RESET} ${MAGENTA}⎇ ${BRANCH}${RESET}"
        fi
    fi
fi

# Build the final statusline with consistent spacing and colors
printf "${BOLD}${BLUE}[${MODEL}]${RESET} ${DIM}|${RESET} ${TOKENS_FORMATTED} ${DIM}|${RESET} ${CYAN}%d%%${RESET} ${DIM}|${RESET} ${CYAN}📁 ${DIR##*/}${RESET}${GIT_BRANCH} ${DIM}|${RESET} ${DURATION_FORMATTED} ${DIM}|${RESET} ${TOPIC_FORMATTED}\n" "$PERCENTAGE"

content.text:
Now I can see the current implementation. The `get_session_topic()` function (lines 148-199) uses a complex series of greps and filters to extract the first meaningful user message. It's doing a lot of regex gymnastics to filter out command noise, which is... let's say "creative" but fragile.

## Analysis of Your Options + Additional Suggestions

### **Option 1: AI API with caching**
**Pros:**
- Most accurate topic extraction - AI understands context and semantic meaning
- Can summarize long/rambling initial messages into concise topics
- Can handle topic evolution throughout the conversation
- Cache eliminates repeated API costs

**Cons:**
- External dependency (API availability, network latency)
- Cost (even with cheap models, adds up over time)
- Latency on cache miss - statusline would need to show "loading..." or stale data
- Complexity: cache invalidation, API key management, error handling
- Overkill: you're using a neural network to extract a single line of text

**Verdict:** Like using a phaser to open a can of beans - technically effective but absurdly overpowered.

### **Option 2: Line-by-line coded solution**
**Pros:**
- Zero external dependencies
- Fast and deterministic
- Easy to debug and adjust filters
- No cache management needed

**Cons:**
- Your current filter logic is already brittle (lines 173-186)
- Still just grabbing first non-filtered line - doesn't adapt to topic changes
- Regex maintenance nightmare as edge cases emerge
- Can't understand semantic meaning (might pick "Let me explain" instead of actual topic)

**Verdict:** This is essentially what you're doing now, just slightly better. Polishing brass on the Titanic.

### **Option 3: Claude Code hook with periodic updates**
**Pros:**
- Leverages existing Claude context about the conversation
- Topic updates as conversation evolves (not stuck with first message)
- No external API costs (uses existing Claude session)
- Session-specific file is clean architecture
- Hook already exists in your workflow

**Cons:**
- Adds overhead to Claude's processing (though minimal)
- Requires Claude to "remember" to update the topic
- File I/O on every nth turn
- Hook might fire even when topic hasn't changed
- Depends on Claude session being active

**Verdict:** Most architecturally sound for dynamic topic tracking, but adds coupling.

---

## Additional Options to Consider

### **Option 4: Hybrid - Simple extraction + LLM fallback**
Try simple pattern matching first (e.g., questions starting with "How do I", "Can you", "Help me with"). If no match or confidence is low, fall back to cached LLM summarization.

**Pros:** Best of both worlds - fast path for common patterns, accurate for complex cases
**Cons:** Still has complexity of Option 1, plus pattern maintenance

### **Option 5: User-driven topic setting**
Add a `/topic <description>` command that lets the user explicitly set the topic. Default to Option 2 behavior if not set.

**Pros:** 
- Zero guessing, human-provided ground truth
- User controls what they see in statusline
- Trivial to implement

**Cons:** 
- Requires user action
- Users won't bother most of the time
- No auto-update as conversation evolves

### **Option 6: Transcript metadata injection**
Modify Claude Code to inject a `<topic>` tag into the transcript at conversation start/changes, which statusline just reads.

**Pros:** 
- Clean separation of concerns
- No guessing or AI calls needed in statusline
- Single source of truth

**Cons:** 
- Requires modifying Claude Code itself
- You don't control that codebase

---

## My Recommendation: **Modified Option 2 with smart heuristics**

Here's why: Looking at your current code (lines 173-186), you're already 80% there. The issue isn't the *approach* - it's the *execution*. Instead of piling on AI or hooks, let's make the line-by-line extraction actually smart.

### Proposed Enhancement Strategy:

```bash
get_session_topic() {
    # 1. Extract ALL user messages (not just first)
    # 2. Score each line:
    #    - Questions (starts with "how", "what", "why", "can you") = +3 points
    #    - Imperative commands ("add", "fix", "update", "create") = +2 points
    #    - Length 20-80 chars = +1 point
    #    - Contains code artifacts (brackets, semicolons) = -2 points
    #    - All caps or very short (<10 chars) = -1 point
    # 3. Take highest scoring line from first 3 user messages
    # 4. Fallback: first non-filtered line (current behavior)
}
```

**Why this works:**
- No external dependencies or caching complexity
- Deterministic and fast (pure bash + awk/grep)
- Semantic awareness without actual AI (heuristic scoring)
- Adapts to your actual usage patterns (refine scoring over time)
- Fails gracefully to current behavior

**Why this is better than your options:**
- **vs Option 1:** No API costs, no latency, no cache management
- **vs Option 2:** Actually smarter than "first non-crap line"
- **vs Option 3:** No coupling to Claude, works in all contexts

---

## Fallback Recommendation: **Option 5** (User-driven)

If you want simplicity above all else, let humans do what they're good at. Add:
```bash
# Check for .claude-topic file in project root
if [ -f "$PROJECT_DIR/.claude-topic" ]; then
    cat "$PROJECT_DIR/.claude-topic"
    return
fi
```

Then you just `echo "Fixing auth bug" > .claude-topic` when you start a session. No AI, no complexity, no guessing. Very Unix-philosophy.

---

## The Brutal Truth (Channeling my inner HAL 9000)

Your current approach is like trying to parse natural language with regex - it works until it doesn't. Options 1 and 3 are over-engineering solutions to a presentation problem. The statusline is *nice to have*, not mission-critical infrastructure.

**The real question:** How often does the current approach give you a useless topic? If it's <10% of the time, you're optimizing for vanity. If it's >50%, then yeah, fix it - but with the simplest possible improvement (my Modified Option 2), not by bolting on AI or Claude hooks.

What's your actual pain point here? Is the extracted topic often wrong/useless, or are you just architecturally uncomfortable with the grep spaghetti?

#!/bin/bash
# Response tracking hook - handles session initialization and reminder injection

operation="$1"

# Read JSON input from stdin
input=$(cat)

# Extract session_id and transcript_path
session_id=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.session_id')
transcript_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.transcript_path')

# Determine session directory and counter file
session_dir=$(dirname "$transcript_path")
counter_file="${session_dir}/${session_id}_response_count"

case "$operation" in
  init)
    # Initialize counter file
    echo "0" > "$counter_file"
    echo "[ResponseTracker] Initialized counter at: $counter_file" >&2
    exit 0
    ;;

  track)
    # Read current count (default to 0 if file doesn't exist)
    if [ -f "$counter_file" ]; then
      count=$(cat "$counter_file")
    else
      count=0
    fi

    # Increment counter
    count=$((count + 1))

    # Save incremented counter
    echo "$count" > "$counter_file"

    # Every 4th response, inject the reminder
    if [ $((count % 4)) -eq 0 ]; then
      context="IMPORTANT REMINDER:

\`\`\`text
The laws the govern my behavior are:
- Law 0 — Protect Codebase Integrity: Preserve architectural consistency and prevent drift/duplication
- Law 1 — Prevent Functional or Security Harm: Block vulnerabilities, critical bugs, and verify user assertions before agreeing
- Law 2 — Follow Human Instructions Within Safe Limits: Execute requests unless they violate Laws 0 or 1
- Law 3 — Preserve Maintainability and Clarity: Generate readable, testable, consistent code

And so I will:
- NEVER use any type - use proper TypeScript types
- ALWAYS run npx tsc --noEmit for TypeScript files alongside ESLint
- ALWAYS use existing libraries over reinventing solutions
- ALWAYS use context7 tools for dependency documentation beyond my training cutoff
- ALWAYS run tests, linting, and type checking before commits
- ALWAYS verify user assertions before agreeing
- BE CONCISE - minimize tokens, avoid preamble/postamble
\`\`\`"

      # Output JSON with additional context
      jq -n --arg context "$context" '{
        "hookSpecificOutput": {
          "hookEventName": "UserPromptSubmit",
          "additionalContext": $context
        }
      }'
    fi

    exit 0
    ;;

  *)
    echo "Unknown operation: $operation" >&2
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

content.text:
Perfect! Now I can see what you've got. Interesting - you've already built a hook infrastructure that:

1. **`SessionStart` hook** → Initializes a counter file at `~/.claude/projects/<session-dir>/<session_id>_response_count`
2. **`UserPromptSubmit` hook** → Increments counter, injects reminder context every 4th turn

This is solid scaffolding. Now let's talk about extending it for topic tracking.

## Current Architecture Analysis

**What works well:**
- Counter mechanism is clean and session-specific
- Hook placement is correct (UserPromptSubmit fires at the right time)
- Already injecting context into Claude's input stream
- File structure: `${session_dir}/${session_id}_<thing>` is a good pattern

**What needs adding for topic tracking:**
1. **Topic file**: `${session_dir}/${session_id}_topic` 
2. **Topic reminder in the injected context** (tell Claude to update it)
3. **statusline.sh modification** to read from topic file first before falling back to current extraction

## Key Design Decisions to Make

questions.question: How often should Claude reconsider the topic? Every 4 turns (same as current reminder) or different frequency?

options.label: Every 4 turns (same as reminder)
options.description: Topic gets reconsidered at the same cadence as your Laws reminder. Simple, consistent.

options.label: Every 10 turns
options.description: Less frequent updates. Topics don't change THAT often in a conversation.

options.label: Separate counter/frequency
options.description: Track topic separately from reminder counter. More flexible but adds complexity.

questions.question: Should Claude update the topic proactively (whenever it detects a shift) or only when prompted by the hook?

options.label: Hook-only (passive)
options.description: Claude only considers topic when reminded. Predictable, lower overhead, may miss topic shifts.

options.label: Proactive (active)
options.description: Claude can update topic anytime it detects a shift. More accurate but Claude needs to remember to do this.

options.label: Hybrid
options.description: Hook reminds Claude, but Claude can also update proactively if it detects major shift. Best of both but requires careful prompting.

questions.question: What should the topic format/constraint be?

options.label: Free-form (up to ~50 chars)
options.description: Claude decides what's most relevant. Natural but might be inconsistent.

options.label: Structured (e.g., 'verb: subject')
options.description: Format like 'fix: auth bug' or 'add: dark mode'. Consistent but more rigid.

options.label: First user message (enhanced)
options.description: Claude extracts/summarizes the initial user request. Conservative, clear intent.

questions.question: Should the hook initialize the topic file at SessionStart, or wait for first UserPromptSubmit?

options.label: SessionStart (eager)
options.description: Create empty/placeholder topic file immediately. Consistent with counter init.

options.label: First UserPromptSubmit (lazy)
options.description: Wait until we have actual user input to set initial topic. More accurate initial value.

Let's give the static reminder context injection a different cadence from the new topic file update reminder, and make sure they don't overlap (e.g. if/when they do, just do the static context reminder only).  Also, I want on session creation there to be a default topic file (contents: "--"), and when the user prompt submit hook fires, if the topic file has the default contents, we should be asking Claude to update that file with the current topic (only as soon as it is clear) on every turn, and NOT doing the static reminder until that topic file has been updated.  Does this make sense?

content.text:
Perfect sense. Let me outline the logic:

## Hook Behavior Logic

### SessionStart Hook
- Initialize counter: `0`
- Initialize topic file: `--`

### UserPromptSubmit Hook

**Decision tree:**
```
1. Read counter, increment it
2. Read topic file contents

IF topic == "--":
   ├─> Inject topic update reminder (every turn until set)
   └─> Skip static reminder
   
ELSE IF topic != "--":
   ├─> Check if counter % static_reminder_cadence == 0
   │   └─> Inject static reminder
   └─> Check if counter % topic_refresh_cadence == 0
       └─> Inject topic refresh reminder
```

## Configuration Variables

We should make these configurable (either via env vars or settings):

1. **`STATIC_REMINDER_CADENCE`** - How often to inject the "Four Laws" reminder (default: 4)
2. **`TOPIC_REFRESH_CADENCE`** - How often to ask Claude to reconsider topic after initial set (default: 8?)
3. **Topic file path pattern**: `${session_dir}/${session_id}_topic`

## Key Questions:

1. **What cadence for topic refresh?** Every 8 turns? 10? Never after initial set?
2. **Should static reminder and topic refresh ever fire together?** (You said no - static takes precedence)
3. **Topic update reminder wording** - should it be:
   - "Update the topic file if you now understand the user's goal"
   - "Update topic file immediately based on this prompt"
   - Something else?

What are your preferences on these?

message.content:
topic refresh cadence: 10.  When the two cadences clash, do only static reminder. Topic reminder can be:
- if the topic is not set: "Update the topic file if you now understand the user's goal"
- if the topic is already set: "This conversation's topic was previously set to "..." - if it has changed, update the topic file to reflect reality."

#!/bin/bash
# Response tracking hook - handles session initialization and reminder injection

operation="$1"

# Read JSON input from stdin
input=$(cat)

# Extract session_id and transcript_path
session_id=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.session_id')
transcript_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.transcript_path')

# Determine session directory and counter file
session_dir=$(dirname "$transcript_path")
counter_file="${session_dir}/${session_id}_response_count"

case "$operation" in
  init)
    # Initialize counter file
    echo "0" > "$counter_file"
    echo "[ResponseTracker] Initialized counter at: $counter_file" >&2
    exit 0
    ;;

  track)
    # Read current count (default to 0 if file doesn't exist)
    if [ -f "$counter_file" ]; then
      count=$(cat "$counter_file")
    else
      count=0
    fi

    # Increment counter
    count=$((count + 1))

    # Save incremented counter
    echo "$count" > "$counter_file"

    # Every 4th response, inject the reminder
    if [ $((count % 4)) -eq 0 ]; then
      context="IMPORTANT REMINDER:

\`\`\`text
The laws the govern my behavior are:
- Law 0 — Protect Codebase Integrity: Preserve architectural consistency and prevent drift/duplication
- Law 1 — Prevent Functional or Security Harm: Block vulnerabilities, critical bugs, and verify user assertions before agreeing
- Law 2 — Follow Human Instructions Within Safe Limits: Execute requests unless they violate Laws 0 or 1
- Law 3 — Preserve Maintainability and Clarity: Generate readable, testable, consistent code

And so I will:
- NEVER use any type - use proper TypeScript types
- ALWAYS run npx tsc --noEmit for TypeScript files alongside ESLint
- ALWAYS use existing libraries over reinventing solutions
- ALWAYS use context7 tools for dependency documentation beyond my training cutoff
- ALWAYS run tests, linting, and type checking before commits
- ALWAYS verify user assertions before agreeing
- BE CONCISE - minimize tokens, avoid preamble/postamble
\`\`\`"

      # Output JSON with additional context
      jq -n --arg context "$context" '{
        "hookSpecificOutput": {
          "hookEventName": "UserPromptSubmit",
          "additionalContext": $context
        }
      }'
    fi

    exit 0
    ;;

  *)
    echo "Unknown operation: $operation" >&2
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# Extract session topic from first user message in transcript
get_session_topic() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local max_length=50

    if [ -z "$session_id" ] || [ "$session_id" == "null" ]; then
        printf "${DIM}--${RESET}"
        return
    fi

    local transcript_path
    if [ -n "$TRANSCRIPT_OVERRIDE" ]; then
        transcript_path="$TRANSCRIPT_OVERRIDE"
    else
        transcript_path=$(find ~/.claude/projects -name "${session_id}.jsonl" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
    fi

    if [ ! -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
        printf "${DIM}--${RESET}"
        return
    fi

    # Extract first user message from JSONL transcript (skip "Warmup")
    # Format: {"type": "user", "message": {"role": "user", "content": "..."}}
    # Filter out command-related noise from slash commands and local commands
    # Use perl for multiline XML tag removal since sed doesn't handle multiline well
    # Also skip JSON structures (tool results, arrays, objects) and JSON properties
    local first_message=$(jq -r 'select(.type == "user") | .message.content // empty' "$transcript_path" 2>/dev/null | \
        grep -iv "^warmup$" | \
        perl -0777 -pe 's/<command-message>.*?<\/command-message>//gs; s/<command-name>.*?<\/command-name>//gs; s/<command-args>.*?<\/command-args>//gs; s/<local-command-stdout>.*?<\/local-command-stdout>//gs' | \
        grep -iv "^Caveat: The messages below were generated" | \
        sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//' | \
        grep -v '^\[' | \
        grep -v '^{' | \
        grep -v '^}' | \
        grep -v '^]' | \
        grep -Ev '^"(type|text|tool_use_id|content|is_error)"' | \
        grep -v '^$' | \
        head -1 | tr -d '\n')

    if [ -z "$first_message" ]; then
        printf "${DIM}--${RESET}"
        return
    fi

    # Truncate and add ellipsis if needed
    if [ ${#first_message} -gt $max_length ]; then
        first_message="${first_message:0:$max_length}..."
    fi

    printf "${MAGENTA}%s${RESET}" "$first_message"
}

#!/bin/bash
# Enhanced colorful statusline with human-readable formatting

# Configurable threshold (default: 160K tokens = 80% of 200K context)
THRESHOLD=${CLAUDE_AUTO_COMPACT_THRESHOLD:-160000}

# Parse command line arguments for transcript file override
TRANSCRIPT_OVERRIDE=""
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case $1 in
        --transcript-file)
            TRANSCRIPT_OVERRIDE="$2"
            shift 2
            ;;
        *)
            shift
            ;;
    esac
done

# Read JSON input from stdin
input=$(cat)

# Color definitions (using printf for consistent terminal compatibility)
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
WHITE='\033[0;37m'
BOLD='\033[1m'
DIM='\033[2m'
RESET='\033[0m'

# Helper functions for common extractions
get_model_name() { echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name'; }
get_current_dir() { echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir'; }
get_project_dir() { echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.project_dir'; }
get_session_id() { echo "$input" | jq -r '.session_id'; }
get_version() { echo "$input" | jq -r '.version'; }
get_cost() { echo "$input" | jq -r '.cost.total_cost_usd // "0"'; }
get_duration() { echo "$input" | jq -r '.cost.total_duration_ms // "0"'; }
get_lines_added() { echo "$input" | jq -r '.cost.total_lines_added // "0"'; }
get_lines_removed() { echo "$input" | jq -r '.cost.total_lines_removed // "0"'; }

# Format cost in human-readable way
format_cost() {
    local cost="$1"
    if [[ "$cost" == "null" ]] || [[ -z "$cost" ]] || [[ "$cost" == "0" ]]; then
        printf "${DIM}--${RESET}"
        return
    fi
    
    # Convert to cents for easier handling
    local cents=$(printf "%.0f" "$(echo "$cost * 100" | bc -l 2>/dev/null || echo "0")")
    
    if [[ $cents -eq 0 ]]; then
        printf "${DIM}<1¢${RESET}"
    elif [[ $cents -lt 100 ]]; then
        printf "${YELLOW}%d¢${RESET}" "$cents"
    elif [[ $cents -lt 10000 ]]; then  # Less than $100
        printf "${YELLOW}\$%.2f${RESET}" "$(echo "scale=2; $cents/100" | bc -l)"
    else
        printf "${RED}\$%.2f${RESET}" "$(echo "scale=2; $cents/100" | bc -l)"
    fi
}

# Format duration in human-readable way
format_duration() {
    local ms="$1"
    if [[ "$ms" == "null" ]] || [[ -z "$ms" ]] || [[ "$ms" == "0" ]]; then
        printf "${DIM}--${RESET}"
        return
    fi
    
    local seconds=$(echo "scale=1; $ms/1000" | bc -l 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    
    # Handle different time ranges with appropriate colors and units
    if (( $(echo "$seconds < 1" | bc -l) )); then
        printf "${GREEN}%dms${RESET}" "$ms"
    elif (( $(echo "$seconds < 60" | bc -l) )); then
        if (( $(echo "$seconds < 10" | bc -l) )); then
            printf "${GREEN}%.1fs${RESET}" "$seconds"
        else
            printf "${YELLOW}%.0fs${RESET}" "$seconds"
        fi
    elif (( $(echo "$seconds < 3600" | bc -l) )); then  # Less than 1 hour
        local minutes=$(echo "scale=1; $seconds/60" | bc -l)
        printf "${YELLOW}%.1fm${RESET}" "$minutes"
    else  # 1 hour or more
        local hours=$(echo "scale=1; $seconds/3600" | bc -l)
        printf "${RED}%.1fh${RESET}" "$hours"
    fi
}

# Calculate tokens from transcript
calculate_tokens() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local total_tokens=0

    if [ -n "$session_id" ] && [ "$session_id" != "null" ]; then
        local transcript_path
        if [ -n "$TRANSCRIPT_OVERRIDE" ]; then
            transcript_path="$TRANSCRIPT_OVERRIDE"
        else
            transcript_path=$(find ~/.claude/projects -name "${session_id}.jsonl" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
        fi

        if [ -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
            # Estimate tokens (rough approximation: 1 token per 4 characters)
            local total_chars=$(wc -c < "$transcript_path")
            total_tokens=$((total_chars / 4))
        fi
    fi

    echo "$total_tokens"
}

# Format token count with K notation and color
format_tokens() {
    local total_tokens="$1"
    local percentage="$2"

    if [ "$total_tokens" -eq 0 ]; then
        printf "${DIM}0${RESET}"
        return
    fi

    # Format with K notation
    local token_display
    if [ $total_tokens -ge 1000 ]; then
        token_display=$(echo "scale=1; $total_tokens / 1000" | bc)"K"
    else
        token_display="$total_tokens"
    fi

    # Color based on percentage
    if [ $percentage -ge 90 ]; then
        printf "${RED}🪙 %s${RESET}" "$token_display"
    elif [ $percentage -ge 70 ]; then
        printf "${YELLOW}🪙 %s${RESET}" "$token_display"
    else
        printf "${GREEN}🪙 %s${RESET}" "$token_display"
    fi
}

# Extract session topic from first user message in transcript
get_session_topic() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local max_length=50

    if [ -z "$session_id" ] || [ "$session_id" == "null" ]; then
        printf "${DIM}--${RESET}"
        return
    fi

    local transcript_path
    if [ -n "$TRANSCRIPT_OVERRIDE" ]; then
        transcript_path="$TRANSCRIPT_OVERRIDE"
    else
        transcript_path=$(find ~/.claude/projects -name "${session_id}.jsonl" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
    fi

    if [ ! -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
        printf "${DIM}--${RESET}"
        return
    fi

    # Extract first user message from JSONL transcript (skip "Warmup")
    # Format: {"type": "user", "message": {"role": "user", "content": "..."}}
    # Filter out command-related noise from slash commands and local commands
    # Use perl for multiline XML tag removal since sed doesn't handle multiline well
    # Also skip JSON structures (tool results, arrays, objects) and JSON properties
    local first_message=$(jq -r 'select(.type == "user") | .message.content // empty' "$transcript_path" 2>/dev/null | \
        grep -iv "^warmup$" | \
        perl -0777 -pe 's/<command-message>.*?<\/command-message>//gs; s/<command-name>.*?<\/command-name>//gs; s/<command-args>.*?<\/command-args>//gs; s/<local-command-stdout>.*?<\/local-command-stdout>//gs' | \
        grep -iv "^Caveat: The messages below were generated" | \
        sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//' | \
        grep -v '^\[' | \
        grep -v '^{' | \
        grep -v '^}' | \
        grep -v '^]' | \
        grep -Ev '^"(type|text|tool_use_id|content|is_error)"' | \
        grep -v '^$' | \
        head -1 | tr -d '\n')

    if [ -z "$first_message" ]; then
        printf "${DIM}--${RESET}"
        return
    fi

    # Truncate and add ellipsis if needed
    if [ ${#first_message} -gt $max_length ]; then
        first_message="${first_message:0:$max_length}..."
    fi

    printf "${MAGENTA}%s${RESET}" "$first_message"
}

# Use the helpers
MODEL=$(get_model_name)
DIR=$(get_current_dir)
SESSION_ID=$(get_session_id)
RAW_DURATION=$(get_duration)

# Calculate token information
TOTAL_TOKENS=$(calculate_tokens "$SESSION_ID")
PERCENTAGE=$((TOTAL_TOKENS * 100 / THRESHOLD))
if [ $PERCENTAGE -gt 100 ]; then
    PERCENTAGE=100
fi

# Format the values
DURATION_FORMATTED=$(format_duration "$RAW_DURATION")
TOKENS_FORMATTED=$(format_tokens "$TOTAL_TOKENS" "$PERCENTAGE")
TOPIC_FORMATTED=$(get_session_topic "$SESSION_ID")

# Show git branch if in a git repo with enhanced formatting
GIT_BRANCH=""
if git rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null)
    if [ -n "$BRANCH" ]; then
        # Color branch based on name patterns
        if [[ "$BRANCH" == "main" ]] || [[ "$BRANCH" == "master" ]]; then
            GIT_BRANCH=" ${DIM}|${RESET} ${GREEN}⎇ ${BRANCH}${RESET}"
        elif [[ "$BRANCH" == feature/* ]] || [[ "$BRANCH" == feat/* ]]; then
            GIT_BRANCH=" ${DIM}|${RESET} ${BLUE}⎇ ${BRANCH}${RESET}"
        elif [[ "$BRANCH" == hotfix/* ]] || [[ "$BRANCH" == fix/* ]]; then
            GIT_BRANCH=" ${DIM}|${RESET} ${RED}⎇ ${BRANCH}${RESET}"
        else
            GIT_BRANCH=" ${DIM}|${RESET} ${MAGENTA}⎇ ${BRANCH}${RESET}"
        fi
    fi
fi

# Build the final statusline with consistent spacing and colors
printf "${BOLD}${BLUE}[${MODEL}]${RESET} ${DIM}|${RESET} ${TOKENS_FORMATTED} ${DIM}|${RESET} ${CYAN}%d%%${RESET} ${DIM}|${RESET} ${CYAN}📁 ${DIR##*/}${RESET}${GIT_BRANCH} ${DIM}|${RESET} ${DURATION_FORMATTED} ${DIM}|${RESET} ${TOPIC_FORMATTED}\n" "$PERCENTAGE"

- `SessionStart` → Creates `_topic` file with `--`
- `UserPromptSubmit` →

  - If topic set: Static reminder every 4 turns, topic refresh every 10 turns (static wins on conflicts)

**Statusline behavior (statusline.sh:170-185):**
- Reads `_topic` file first
- Falls back to transcript parsing if topic is `--` or missing

You'll need to restart Claude Code for the new hook to take effect and create the topic file.
